name: Seldon Powell
abstract: Seldon Powell (November 15, 1928 – January 25, 1997) was an American tenor saxophonist and flautist whose work spanned multiple genres, including jazz and rhythm and blues.

concerts.label: New York Jazz Repertory Company: The Music of Quincy Jones
Kool Jazz Festival: Benny Carter and Illinois Jacquet

about_quotes.speaker: Norris Turney
about_quotes.evidence: Hank Jones was on piano, Seldon Powell on tenor, I played alto and clarinet. Richard Jones played trumpet. Nice little band, yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norris Turney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: In that band, originally, was Frank Wess, Seldon Powell replaced saxophonist, Hubert Laws.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Most
about_quotes.evidence: I think it was my friend again, Arnold Rosen, he said, “Why don’t you get up there and play?” Anyhow I sat in with Buddy and he says, “You got the gig.” ... Seldon Powell was leaving.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Anne Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: I just remember so many of them that were on these sessions... There were Seldon Powell
about_quotes.doc_title: Anne Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2025
